_metadata.title: BNS (Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023) – Complete Bail Classification
_metadata.source: First Schedule, Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), 2023
_metadata.note: Sourced from official NCRB/India Code schedule. 'Cognizable' = police may arrest without warrant. 'Non-cognizable' = police shall NOT arrest without warrant. Bail status is per the First Schedule of BNSS.

antil_categories.A: Offences punishable with imprisonment of less than 7 years
antil_categories.B: Offences punishable with imprisonment of 7 years or more but less than life imprisonment or death
antil_categories.C: Offences punishable with life imprisonment or death

49.offence: Abetment of any offence, if the act abetted is committed in consequence, and where no express provision is made for its punishment
49.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
49.is_bailable: According as offence abetted is bailable or non-bailable
49.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

50.offence: Abetment of any offence, if the person abetted does act with different intention from that of abettor
50.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
50.is_bailable: According as offence abetted is bailable or non-bailable
50.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

51.offence: Abetment of any offence, when one act is abetted and a different act is done; subject to proviso
51.punishment: Same as for offence intended to be abetted
51.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
51.is_bailable: According as offence abetted is bailable or non-bailable
51.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

52.offence: Abettor when liable to cumulative punishment for act abetted and for act done
52.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
52.is_bailable: According as offence abetted is bailable or non-bailable
52.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

53.offence: Abetment of any offence, when an effect is caused by the act abetted different from that intended by the abettor
53.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
53.is_bailable: According as offence abetted is bailable or non-bailable
53.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

54.offence: Abetment of any offence, if abettor present when offence is committed
54.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
54.is_bailable: According as offence abetted is bailable or non-bailable
54.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

55.offence: Abetment of an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, if the offence be not committed in consequence of the abetment
55.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
55.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

56.offence: Abetment of an offence punishable with imprisonment, if the offence be not committed in consequence of the abetment
56.punishment: Imprisonment extending to one-fourth of longest term provided for offence, or fine, or both
56.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
56.is_bailable: According as offence abetted is bailable or non-bailable
56.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

57.offence: Abetting commission of an offence by the public or by more than ten persons
57.punishment: Imprisonment which may extend to 7 years and fine
57.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
57.is_bailable: According as offence abetted is bailable or non-bailable
57.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

58_a.offence: Concealing design to commit offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, if the offence be committed
58_a.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
58_a.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

58_b.offence: Concealing design to commit offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, if the offence be not committed
58_b.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
58_b.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

59_a.offence: A public servant concealing a design to commit an offence which it is his duty to prevent, if the offence be committed
59_a.punishment: Imprisonment extending to one-half of longest term provided for offence, or fine, or both
59_a.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
59_a.is_bailable: According as offence abetted is bailable or non-bailable
59_a.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

59_b.offence: Public servant concealing design – if the offence be punishable with death or imprisonment for life
59_b.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
59_b.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

59_c.offence: Public servant concealing design – if the offence be not committed
59_c.punishment: Imprisonment extending to one-fourth of longest term provided for offence, or fine, or both
59_c.cognizable: According as offence abetted is cognizable or non-cognizable
59_c.triable_by: Court by which offence abetted is triable

61_2_a.offence: Criminal conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for a term of 2 years or upwards
61_2_a.punishment: Same as for abetment of the offence which is the object of the conspiracy
61_2_a.cognizable: According as the offence which is the object of conspiracy is cognizable or non-cognizable
61_2_a.is_bailable: According as offence which is object of conspiracy is bailable or non-bailable
61_2_a.triable_by: Court by which abetment of the offence which is the object of conspiracy is triable

61_2_b.punishment: Imprisonment for 6 months, or fine, or both

62.offence: Attempting to commit offence punishable with imprisonment for life, or imprisonment, and in such attempt doing any act towards the commission of the offence
62.punishment: One half of the imprisonment for life, or imprisonment not exceeding one-half of the longest term provided for the offence, or fine, or both
62.cognizable: According as the offence is cognizable or non-cognizable
62.is_bailable: According as the offence attempted by the offender is bailable or non-bailable
62.triable_by: The court by which the offence attempted is triable

64_1.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for not less than 10 years but which may extend to imprisonment for life, and fine

64_2.offence: Rape by a police officer or public servant or member of armed forces or person in management/staff of jail/remand home/hospital, or rape committed by person in a position of trust or authority, or by a near relative
64_2.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for not less than 10 years but which may extend to imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life) and fine

65_1.offence: Rape on a woman under sixteen years of age
65_1.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for not less than 20 years but which may extend to imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life) and fine

65_2.offence: Rape on a woman under twelve years of age
65_2.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for not less than 20 years but which may extend to imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life) and fine, or death

66.offence: Rape inflicting injury which causes death or causes the woman to be in a persistent vegetative state
66.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for not less than 20 years but which may extend to imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life) or death

67.offence: Sexual intercourse by husband upon his wife during separation
67.punishment: Imprisonment for not less than 2 years but which may extend to 7 years and fine

68.offence: Sexual intercourse by a person in authority, etc.
68.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for not less than 5 years but which may extend to 10 years and fine

69.offence: Sexual intercourse by employing deceitful means, etc. (false promise of marriage, suppressed identity, etc.)
69.punishment: Imprisonment which may extend to 10 years and fine

70_1.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for not less than 20 years but which may extend to imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life) and fine

70_2.offence: Gang rape on a woman under eighteen years of age
70_2.punishment: Imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life) and fine, or death

71.punishment: Imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life) or death

72_1.offence: Disclosure of identity of the victim of certain offences, etc.

73.offence: Printing or publication of a proceeding without prior permission of court

74.offence: Assault or use of criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
74.punishment: Imprisonment for 1 year which may extend to 5 years and fine

75_2.offence: Sexual harassment (clauses i, ii, iii of sub-section 1)
75_2.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment with 3 years, or fine, or both

75_3.punishment: Imprisonment for 1 year, or fine, or both

76.offence: Assault or use of criminal force to woman with intent to disrobe
76.punishment: Imprisonment for not less than 3 years but which may extend to 7 years and fine

77.punishment: Imprisonment for not less than 1 year but which may extend to 3 years and fine (first conviction). Imprisonment 3–7 years on subsequent conviction.

78_2.punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine (first conviction). Imprisonment up to 5 years and fine (subsequent conviction).

79.offence: Uttering any word or making any gesture intended to insult the modesty of a woman, etc.
79.punishment: Simple imprisonment for 3 years and fine

80_2.punishment: Imprisonment for not less than 7 years but which may extend to imprisonment for life

81.offence: A man by deceit causing a woman not lawfully married to him to believe that she is lawfully married to him and to cohabit with him in that belief

82_1.offence: Marrying again during the lifetime of a husband or wife

82_2.offence: Same offence with concealment of the former marriage from the person with whom subsequent marriage is contracted

83.offence: A person with fraudulent intention going through the ceremony of being married, knowing that he is not thereby lawfully married
83.punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

84.offence: Enticing or taking away or detaining with a criminal intent a married woman
84.punishment: Imprisonment for 2 years, or fine, or both

85.offence: Punishment for subjecting a married woman to cruelty (by husband or relative)
85.cognizable: Cognizable (only if information given by aggrieved person or blood/marriage relative to officer-in-charge of police station)

87.offence: Kidnapping, abducting or inducing woman to compel her marriage, etc.

88.punishment: Imprisonment for 3 years, or fine, or both. If woman be quick with child: Imprisonment for 7 years and fine.

89.punishment: Imprisonment for life, or imprisonment for 10 years and fine

103_1.punishment: Death or imprisonment for life and fine

103_2.offence: Murder by group of five or more persons
103_2.punishment: Death or imprisonment for life and fine

104.punishment: Death or imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life)

105.offence: Culpable homicide not amounting to murder
105.punishment: Imprisonment for life, or imprisonment for not less than 5 years but which may extend to 10 years and fine (if done with intention of causing death). Imprisonment for 10 years and fine (if done with knowledge).

106_1.punishment: Imprisonment for 5 years and fine. For registered medical practitioner: Imprisonment for 2 years and fine.

106_2.offence: Causing death by rash and negligent driving of vehicle and escaping

107.offence: Abetment of suicide of child or person of unsound mind, etc.
107.punishment: Death, or imprisonment for life, or imprisonment for 10 years and fine

109_1.punishment: Imprisonment for 10 years and fine. If such act causes hurt to any person: Imprisonment for life, or as above.

115_2.punishment: Imprisonment for 1 year or fine of Rs. 10,000, or both

117_3.offence: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt resulting in permanent disability or persistent vegetative state
117_3.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for not less than 10 years but which may extend to imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life)

303_1.punishment: Imprisonment for 3 years, or fine, or both

303_2.offence: Theft having made preparation for causing death, hurt, restraint or fear of death or hurt
303_2.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for not less than 1 year but which may extend to 10 years and fine

304.punishment: Imprisonment for 3 years, or fine, or both
304.note: New offence introduced in BNS (no IPC equivalent)

305.offence: Theft in dwelling house, or means of transportation, or place of worship, or after preparation for causing hurt

306.offence: Theft by clerk or servant of property in possession of master

307.offence: Theft after preparation made for causing death or hurt or restraint
307.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for not less than 1 year but which may extend to 10 years and fine

308_1.punishment: Imprisonment for 3 years, or fine, or both

308_2.offence: Putting a person in fear of death or grievous hurt in order to commit extortion

309_1.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for not less than 3 years but which may extend to 10 years and fine

309_2.offence: Robbery on highway between sunset and sunrise

310_1.punishment: Imprisonment for life, or rigorous imprisonment for 10 years and fine

310_2.punishment: Death or imprisonment for life and fine

318_1.punishment: Imprisonment for 3 years, or fine, or both

318_2.offence: Cheating a person whose interest the offender was bound, either by law or by legal contract, to protect
318_2.punishment: Imprisonment for 5 years, or fine, or both

318_3.punishment: Imprisonment for 5 years, or fine, or both

318_4.offence: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property or alteration of valuable security

319_1.offence: Fraudulent or dishonest disposal of property by person intrusted with it for specified purpose
319_1.punishment: Imprisonment for 3 years, or fine, or both

320.punishment: Imprisonment for 3 years, or fine, or both

321.punishment: Imprisonment for life, or imprisonment for 10 years and fine

324_1.punishment: Imprisonment for 6 months, or fine, or both

351_1.punishment: Imprisonment for 2 years, or fine, or both

351_2.offence: Criminal intimidation by anonymous communication or after concealment of name
351_2.punishment: Imprisonment for 2 years (in addition to punishment under sub-section 1), or fine, or both

351_3.offence: Criminal intimidation to cause death or grievous hurt, etc.
351_3.punishment: Imprisonment for 7 years, or fine, or both

352.offence: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of the peace
352.punishment: Imprisonment for 2 years, or fine, or both

354_1.punishment: Simple imprisonment for 2 years, or fine, or both
